commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Trace and explain render phase using a new example and justify the result.
Explain reconciliation work using a new example and justify the result.
Trace and explain commit phase using a new example and justify the result.
Apply dom updates using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Render phase** → **Reconciliation work** → **Commit phase** → **DOM updates**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Render phase.
choices.label: React often colocates JSX with the state and behavior that determine it. Concerns are separated by component responsibility rather than file type alone.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Render phase.
choices.label: Reuse may come from functions, custom Hooks, composition, configurable components, or domain abstractions.

choices.feedback: Render phase is best understood as follows: During render, React calls components to calculate the next UI. Render must remain pure because React may repeat, pause, or discard the work.
choices.label: During render, React calls components to calculate the next UI. Render must remain pure because React may repeat, pause, or discard the work.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Render phase.
choices.label: A component boundary defines ownership of markup, behavior, and dependencies. Good boundaries minimize reasons to change.

choices.feedback: Reconciliation work is best understood as follows: Reconciliation compares previous and next element trees using type, position, and keys to decide which instances are preserved or replaced.

choices.feedback: Commit phase is best understood as follows: During commit, React applies calculated DOM changes and updates refs. Commit is the point where host mutations become observable.

choices.feedback: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.
choices.label: First identify the requirement, then apply the relevant rule from Render phase, inspect the result, and only then refactor or optimize.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Render and Commit Phases.
choices.label: Optimize first, then decide what the code is supposed to do.

choices.feedback: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.
choices.label: Use Render and Commit Phases when it makes intent, ownership, or data flow clearer; avoid adding it mechanically when a simpler, equally explicit solution is sufficient.

sections.body: During render, React calls components to calculate the next UI. Render must remain pure because React may repeat, pause, or discard the work.
sections.title: Render phase

sections.body: Reconciliation compares previous and next element trees using type, position, and keys to decide which instances are preserved or replaced.
sections.title: Reconciliation work

sections.body: During commit, React applies calculated DOM changes and updates refs. Commit is the point where host mutations become observable.
sections.title: Commit phase

sections.body: React updates host nodes according to reconciliation. A component render does not imply every descendant DOM node is replaced.
sections.title: DOM updates

sections.explanation: The component calculates output during render; React applies changes during commit.
sections.title: Worked example

summary: Render and Commit Phases connects Render phase, Reconciliation work, Commit phase.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Render and Commit Phases

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: The ideas in render and commit phases recur throughout later React work, reducing memorization and debugging later. It belongs to the **How React Renders** module.
